title: elaeocarpus tonkinensis
intro: Elaeocarpus tonkinensis, also known as the Red Sandalwood or Red Ebony, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Elaeocarpus tonkinensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
